items.title: Ethereum Foundation hits ‘Glamsterdam’ milestones, names new protocol leads
items.summary: The Ethereum Foundation has finalized a new gas limit floor and improvement proposal for its “Glamsterdam” upgrade, which is likely to go live sometime in the third quarter of 2026.

items.title: The biggest consensus overhaul in Solana history is officially live for testing
items.summary: The upgrade, known as Alpenglow, is live on a community test cluster, said Solana core developer Anza.

items.title: DAPPOS launches xBubble: An AI agent that learns and uses AI for you
items.summary: xBubble allows users to complete specific tasks with simpler prompts by automatically building and dispatching task-specific AI agents.

items.title: Why the TradFi takeover of crypto might not be the death blow analysts expect
items.summary: Gate’s CBO argues that Bloomberg’s warning of a TradFi takeover is "oversimplified," noting that global exchanges evolved past fee-only models years ago.

items.title: Banking groups escalate fight over stablecoin yield ahead of Senate vote
items.summary: The American Bankers Association amplified warnings the Senate’s Clarity Act could push deposit flight into stablecoins unless lawmakers tighten yield limits.

items.title: Bitcoin whipsaws on CME open as Iran tensions pressure crypto markets
items.summary: BTC briefly topped $82,400 before slipping below $81,000 as traders repositioned around CME futures open and geopolitical risks rattled markets.

items.title: Seven Democrats seen as ‘key’ to advancing CLARITY Act: Galaxy
items.summary: The CLARITY Act, introduced in July 2025, stalled in January after Coinbase withdrew its support for the legislation over concerns about legal protections and stablecoin yields.

items.title: Trailmark turns code into graphs
items.summary: We’re open-sourcing Trailmark, a library that parses source code into a queryable call graph of functions, classes, call relationships, and semantic metadata, then exposes that graph through a Python API that Claude skills can call directly. Install it now: uv pip install trailma

items.title: Aave launches binding Arbitrum vote to move $71 million in disputed ETH
items.summary: Arbitrum delegates begin the binding governance process to transfer disputed exploit funds to Aave, while North Korean terrorism creditors continue fighting for ownership in Manhattan court

items.title: 'Bitcoin transactions can be monitored’: Ray Dalio explains why central banks won’t touch BTC
items.summary: Ray Dalio joins the privacy debate, saying Bitcoin’s full transparency makes it less likely to be adopted by central banks.

items.title: Ripple-linked XRP holds near $1.46 as breakout attempt fades
items.summary: XRP briefly pushed toward $1.49 on heavy volume before stalling again near a resistance zone that has capped rallies for months.

items.title: Bitcoin briefly hits $82,000, SOL, DOGE higher as Michael Burry warns of stock crash
items.summary: Bitcoin held above $81,000 after touching $82,026 overnight, while solana led the major altcoins on the week as The Big Short investor warned the Nasdaq 100 had reached dot-com bubble territory and oil zoomed past $105 on fresh Iran ceasefire doubts.

items.title: Clarity Act, in the flesh, unveiled by U.S. Senate Banking Committee before hearing
items.summary: The crypto market structure bill had already been making the industry rounds behind closed doors, but the lawmakers have released the text before their vote.

items.title: Bitcoin’s floor looks firmer at $80,000, but traders still don’t trust the breakout
items.summary: BTC has recovered from Friday’s jobs-driven dip, but Enflux says overhead resistance remains intact while Glassnode’s market structure data suggests traders are buying the rally while still positioning for downside.

items.title: ‘I failed them’: Goliath Ventures CEO charged with crypto Ponzi apologizes
items.summary: Christopher Delgado, the former Goliath Ventures CEO charged with fraud and money laundering, has publicly apologized to investors of what US prosecutors allege is a Ponzi scheme.

items.title: Bitmine slows Ethereum buys, targets December to own 5% of supply
items.summary: Tom Lee doubles down on the crypto spring, points to the Ether price rising in correlation with software stocks as further evidence.

items.title: Hackers used AI to craft zero-day attack to bypass 2FA: Google
items.summary: Google’s Threat Intelligence Group says it has “high confidence” a threat actor used an AI model to help discover and weaponize a vulnerability in a popular system admin tool.

items.title: MARA shares sink on first-quarter revenue miss, $1.3B loss
items.summary: MARA Holdings says Bitcoin mining remains the company’s “operational foundation” while it moves to expand into AI.

items.title: Bitcoin funding rates turn positive: Is BTC rally to $85K next?
items.summary: Bitcoin’s funding rate turned positive as the cryptocurrency held the $80,000 level. Will an uptick in spot ETF inflows trigger a rally to $85,000?

items.title: 'A big nothing burger': A Q&A with Strategy's Michael Saylor on selling bitcoin
items.summary: Michael Saylor sat down with CoinDesk on selling bitcoin for dividends, retiring debt with STRC proceeds, and why critics who say Strategy buys the weekly top are missing the point.
